value: /Users/davidcruwys/dev/ad/apps/dark-factory/ylo-experiment/runs/b65/transcript.txt

A tutorial demonstrating how to use Claude Code and context engineering to automate a lead generation and outreach workflow for a public speaking coach (Guy Monroe), specifically building a system to research, evaluate, and generate presence reports for 150 plastic surgeon prospects using web searches, structured data, and iterative prompt refinement.
context engineering
Claude Code
public speaking coach
lead generation automation
plastic surgeons outreach
AI marketing workflow
prompt engineering
presence report
scorecard assessment
charisma coach
web search automation
HTML report generation
CSV data processing
expert coaching
sales outreach
Content targets expert coaches and consultants seeking outreach automation tools
Primary audience includes charisma coaches and public speaking coaches (exemplified by Guy Munro)
Secondary audience targets plastic surgeons as prospective clients or leads
Audience members are assumed to have done manual prompt engineering work (e.g., in ChatGPT) before seeking automation
Audience skill level ranges from semi-technical (comfortable with spreadsheets, ChatGPT prompting) to non-technical (need guidance on Claude Code permissions)
Content addresses business development professionals wanting to generate 150+ warm leads
Audience members may have domain-specific scoring frameworks or evaluation rubrics they want to automate
Viewers are expected to be unfamiliar with context engineering but open to learning it
Audience includes solo practitioners or small consultancy operators, not enterprise teams
Content appeals to people who do one-to-one coaching and want to scale outreach without hiring staff

150 target plastic surgeons in a spreadsheet; 4 test surgeons found (2 from Sydney AU: Jeremy Hunt and Ellis Choi; 2 from London: Jan Steneck and Sijatha Tadapathy); 3 workflow steps (research, scorecard, assessment report); 36% context window remaining noted during session; 2 files initially created (fact sheet and presence report) later combined into 1; planned 150 hot leads in a couple of days

Context engineering can automate repetitive expert outreach workflows, enabling one person to research and evaluate 150+ leads systematically
Structuring AI-assisted workflows around iterative prompt improvement creates a self-correcting system that gets better with each processed record
Separating raw source data from working files and maintaining a living CLAUDE.md system prompt keeps AI agents oriented as the project evolves
Combining automated web research with human expert scoring (scorecard) creates a hybrid workflow that preserves expert judgment while removing manual data gathering
Building test datasets from public records allows safe iteration of prompts before running them on real target prospects
Fact sheets and presence reports work best as a single combined output rather than separate files, minimizing context switching during human review
Allowing AI to identify and flag data gaps in its own outputs enables continuous prompt refinement without manual audit overhead
A hierarchical folder structure with indexed naming conventions makes large batches of AI-generated research files easy to navigate and reference

Should it update the workflow diagram?

Is there, would you like help with TikTok or social media or online sort of presence?
Real-world client collaboration with a charisma coach (Guy Monroe) as the subject matter expert, grounding the workflow in an actual business use case rather than a hypothetical
Live demonstration of iterative prompt refinement — watching Claude Code improve its own prompts based on output gaps discovered during processing, not just using pre-written prompts
Context Engineering approach using Claude Code and CLAUDE.md as the system prompt anchor, showing how to maintain and evolve the AI context document as the project grows
Hybrid workflow design that combines human expert judgment (Guy manually scoring charisma/public speaking) with automated AI research and report generation, making the automation non-prescriptive
Anti-template philosophy — deliberately avoiding rigid output templates to allow the LLM to adapt its output format to each subject, while using formatting guidelines instead
End-to-end pipeline construction in a single session, from raw CSV to structured per-surgeon folders with research, scorecard, and presence report stages
Using test surgeons (publicly available, internationally diverse) as a safe iteration sandbox before applying the workflow to the actual 150-target list
Gap analysis as a first-class workflow step — the system documents what information is missing and feeds that back into prompt improvement rather than silently skipping it
